name: Raguel
description: Raguel, whose name translates to "Friend of God" or "Shepherd of God," is an archangel primarily recognized within certain apocryphal and pseudepigraphal texts of the Abrahamic traditions, particularly in the Book of Enoch. Raguel's principal role is the enforcement of divine justice and the maintenance of order within the angelic realms. He is often depicted as an angel of vengeance, ensuring that fallen angels and other transgressors face retribution for their misdeeds. Unlike more widely recognized archangels such as Michael or Gabriel, Raguel's presence is less prominent in mainstream religious texts. However, his function as a celestial enforcer underscores the Abrahamic emphasis on divine judgment and the restoration of cosmic equilibrium. His association with justice and harmony makes him a significant figure in understanding the complex hierarchies and responsibilities within angelic lore. Raguel is also viewed as a protector of goodness and righteousness, actively working to thwart evil and uphold God's divine plan. Some traditions also associate him with the element of fire, symbolizing purification and divine wrath.
subtitle: Angel of Justice and Harmony

keyMyths.title: Raguel's Judgment of Fallen Angels
keyMyths.description: In the Book of Enoch, Raguel is charged with taking vengeance on the luminaries who have transgressed God's laws. These fallen angels corrupted humanity and disrupted the natural order. Raguel, acting as God's enforcer, metes out punishment to these celestial beings, ensuring that they are held accountable for their actions and restoring balance to the cosmos. This myth highlights Raguel's role as a divine judge and his commitment to upholding God's righteous decrees.

keyMyths.title: The Restoration of Heavenly Harmony
keyMyths.description: Raguel's interventions are not limited to punishment; he also actively works to restore harmony within the heavenly realms. When discord or chaos threatens the celestial order, Raguel steps in to mediate and restore equilibrium. This myth emphasizes his role as a shepherd, guiding and protecting the angels under his charge and ensuring that they remain aligned with God's will. His actions prevent further rebellion and maintain the integrity of the divine plan.

keyMyths.title: Raguel's Role in Protecting the Righteous
keyMyths.description: Beyond punishing the wicked, Raguel is also seen as a protector of the righteous. He shields those who are faithful to God from the influence of evil and guides them on the path of righteousness. This aspect of Raguel's character highlights his compassionate side and his commitment to supporting those who strive to live according to divine principles. He serves as a guardian, ensuring that the faithful are rewarded and protected from harm.

keyMyths.title: Raguel and the Element of Fire
keyMyths.description: Some interpretations associate Raguel with the element of fire, symbolizing purification and divine wrath. This connection underscores his role as an agent of divine justice, using fire to cleanse the world of corruption and punish those who defy God's laws. The fiery aspect of Raguel's character emphasizes the intensity of his commitment to upholding righteousness and his unwavering dedication to enforcing divine judgment.

sources.author: R.H. Charles
sources.description: A comprehensive translation and commentary on the Book of Enoch, a primary source for understanding Raguel's role.
sources.text: A comprehensive translation and commentary on the Book of Enoch, a primary source for understanding Raguel's role.
